ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic brioche entry (FOODON_03315091). No specific entry exists for walnut-pistachio cream-cheese filled brioche; closest_parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — enriched/sweet bread category
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for filled brioche with nut toppings. A000E (bread and similar products) is the closest defensible anchor. closest_parent match used. Filled enriched bread sub-classification applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific filled brioche product; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one brioche bun of approximately 80 g, as commonly sold in Singaporean bakeries.

nutrient_source.title: Walnut & Pistachio Cheese Brioche — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Walnut & Pistachio Cheese Brioche (cr_id G00011001). All per-100g macros, sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, and cholesterol values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are not reported in the HPB source and are set to -1 except where estimated from USDA analogous data for cream-cheese-filled enriched bread.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate: white bread / enriched sweet bread GI reference
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for walnut-pistachio cream-cheese brioche. GI estimated at 72 (high) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white enriched sweet bread / brioche-type products (GI range 70–75). Glycemic load per 80 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (72 × 7.08) / 100 ≈ 5.1, rounded to 5.0. The cream cheese filling and nut toppings may modestly reduce the effective GI in practice, but the refined flour base dominates the glycaemic response.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1279 mg per 100 g — more than double the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this product; a single 80 g serving delivers approximately 1023 mg sodium, representing over 45% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined brioche base (GI ≈ 72) with zero dietary fibre produces rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid this product because the refined flour base with no fibre creates a high glycaemic load, and the saturated fat from cream cheese and butter further impairs insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (estimated ≈ 8.5 g/100 g from cream cheese and butter) combined with critically elevated sodium (1279 mg/100 g) and cholesterol (78 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this product; the combination of high saturated fat, high sodium, and moderate dietary cholesterol substantially elevates LDL-cholesterol and blood pressure risk, while the absence of dietary fibre provides no mitigating benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Extremely high sodium (1279 mg/100 g) worsens RA-related tissue oedema; saturated fat from cream cheese and butter is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product; the very high sodium content exacerbates joint swelling and inflammation, and the saturated fat profile negates the modest anti-inflammatory benefit of the walnut and pistachio content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Walnut and Pistachio Cheese Brioche is a Singapore bakery product made using the sponge-dough method, producing a soft, rich, and chewy enriched butter bread. The interior is filled with a specially blended pistachio cream cheese that delivers a pronounced pistachio flavour and a creamy, slightly tangy aroma. The surface is decorated with whole walnuts, crushed pistachios, and cocoa almond paste, creating a visually striking and texturally complex product. Key ingredients include wheat flour, butter, eggs, sugar, salt, cream cheese, pistachio, walnut, cocoa powder, and almond paste. Nutritionally, the product is calorie-dense at 307 kcal per 100 g, with a notably high protein content of 16.09 g/100 g (partly attributable to the cream cheese filling), high fat at 23.06 g/100 g, and a relatively low carbohydrate content of 8.85 g/100 g. However, sodium is critically elevated at 1279 mg/100 g, making this product unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The absence of dietary fibre and the high-GI refined flour base make it a poor choice for people with Type 2 Diabetes. Calcium is exceptionally high at 1375 mg/100 g, likely reflecting the cream cheese component. This product is sold in Singaporean bakeries and is typically consumed as a snack or light meal. It is vegetarian but not vegan, not certified halal, and contains major allergens including gluten, dairy, eggs, and tree nuts.
common_user_queries: What is walnut pistachio cheese brioche?
Is brioche with cream cheese healthy?
Calories in pistachio cheese brioche Singapore
Is walnut pistachio brioche high in sodium?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00011001, confidence 0.95 in source record). The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore dishes. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA analogous data for cream-cheese-filled enriched bread and documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for enriched sweet bread, which is the closest approved GI source available.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB; this is confirmed zero per the HPB source record and is plausible given the refined flour base with no whole-grain component. 2. saturated_fat_g (8.5 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for cream-cheese-filled enriched bread (USDA SR Legacy item 'Cheese bread, commercially prepared') as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions for this item. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, water_g, and all vitamin fields set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, moisture, and vitamin data for this specific Singapore bakery product are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 4. calcium_mg at 1375 mg/100 g is unusually high for a bread product; this value is taken verbatim from the HPB source and likely reflects the cream cheese filling component. Independent verification is recommended. 5. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for white enriched sweet bread / brioche-type products; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. 6. Halal status is not confirmed; the product contains dairy and eggs and may contain non-halal ingredients depending on the specific manufacturer.
